items.title: VI Live: FC Groningen gaat met voorsprong rusten tegen PSV
items.summary: De Eredivisie-afsluiter is dit weekend voor FC Groningen en koploper PSV. Zij staan om 16.45 uur tegenover elkaar. Volg de wedstrijd op de voet in deze VI Live.

items.title: Echteld prijst scorende Goes: 'Leider die laat zien waar hij van gemaakt is'
items.summary: Hoewel Lee-Roy Echteld baalde van de late remise tegen Ajax had de interim-trainer van AZ lovende woorden over voor Wouter Goes. Na afloop sprak hij met verslaggever Kalum van Oudheusden.

items.title: VI Live: kraker tussen Liverpool en Man City van start
items.summary: Zondag staat ons een fraaie voetbaldag te wachten. De duels van Feyenoord, Ajax en PSV volgen we in aparte liveblogs, maar in deze VI Live blijf je op de hoogte van alle andere duels. Bijvoorbeeld van de Engelse topper tussen Liverpool en Manchester City.

items.title: Duel tussen Cambuur en Emmen voor de derde keer afgelast
items.summary: Voor de derde keer gaat er een streep door het duel tussen FC Emmen en SC Cambuur. Omdat een deel van het veld van De Oude Meerdijk nog is bevroren, kan de ontmoeting ook maandag niet doorgaan.

items.title: Crystal Palace end long wait for win after Ismaïla Sarr’s strike stuns Brighton
items.summary: Fabian Hürzeler must be wondering if he will ever have another chance to get one over Oliver Glasner. The Brighton head coach’s record against his Crystal Palace counterpart and someone he counts as a mentor now reads three defeats and a solitary draw after Ismaïla Sarr’s second-half goal ended Palace’s 12-match winless run in satisfying style against their arch-rivals. But while Glasner can breathe slightly more easily after new signings Evann Guessand and Jørgen Strand Larsen both made an impact on their debuts, Hürzeler left the field to widespread boos from his own fans and chants of “You’re getting sacked in the morning”. It is now only one win in their past 12 league games and the German, who became the youngest manager in Premier League, is facing the real possibility of being dragged into a relegation battle. Continue reading...

items.title: Is Pep Guardiola getting ready to leave Man City? Only he knows
items.summary: It wouldn't surprise anybody if Guardiola steps down as Man City manager at the end of the season.

items.title: Premier League live updates: Liverpool host title-chasing Man City
Makkelie legt uit waarom hij tweede AZ-goal afkeurde
items.summary: Danny Makkelie heeft verteld waarom hij zondagmiddag een streep zette door de tweede goal van AZ. Troy Parrott dacht de 2-0 te maken, maar na advies van de VAR werd de goal geannuleerd.

items.title: Liverpool v Manchester City: Premier League – live
items.summary: Minute-by-minute coverage of Sunday’s clash at Anfield Jonathan Wilson: no longer the big show | Email Michael This is a good read. Arne Slot, Liverpool manager, is next to speak to Sky Sports. He reiterates that the biggest recent improvement that his side have made is their work rate out of possession. We have made a lot of steps forward mainly off the ball, mainly with players better able to play this schedule we are playing. The biggest improvement I see is players are more ready for the combination of the Premier League and Champions League at the intensity we need. We have both shown we are good in ball possession and can create chances. Both teams want the ball as much as possible and both have shown from a high press they are a threat as well. A good and proper counter attack can be dangerous at both ends as well. Continue reading...

items.title: Bucks' Antetokounmpo invests in Chelsea Women
items.summary: NBA star Giannis Antetokounmpo has announced he has joined the ownership group of Chelsea Women which includes his "friend" Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian.

items.title: Liverpool signing Jacquet suffers 'serious' injury
items.summary: Liverpool 's new summer signing Jérémy Jacquet has sustained a "serious" shoulder injury, according to Rennes head coach Habib Beye.

items.title: Foxborough demands clarity on $8M WC funding
items.summary: Town officials in Foxborough, Massachusetts, are seeking clarity on the source of around $8 million they say is needed to fund public safety for this summer's World Cup games at Gillette Stadium, the home of the New England Patriots.

items.title: Palace break winless run as they beat booed-off Brighton
items.summary: Crystal Palace ended a 12-game winless run in all competitions with a 1-0 victory over rivals Brighton, whose head coach Fabian Hurzeler came under fire from the home fans.

items.title: England show steel as Nepal prove they are not to be underestimated
items.summary: When Adil Rashid is getting mowed into the stands, you know you have a problem.

items.title: Championship talking points: Confidence could be Coventry's issue as Ipswich crank up the pressure
Reed wins second title in three weeks at Qatar Masters
items.summary: Patrick Reed continued his great start to the 2026 season with a two-stroke victory at the Qatar Masters.

items.title: Arsenal put brakes on Manchester City’s WSL title surge thanks to Smith strike
items.summary: This 1-0 defeat of Manchester City at the Emirates Stadium was never going to be a statement of ­Arsenal’s title intentions – City’s lead at the top is just too big – but a result against the likely champions still had huge value, mentally and in terms of the race for Champions League football. It would take the most almighty of collapses for City to lose their grip on a second league title, 10 years after their first. They are still eight points clear of ­Manchester United and 10 ahead of Arsenal, who have a game in hand over those around them. Continue reading...

items.title: Smith scores winner as Arsenal halt Man City WSL title charge
items.summary: Olivia Smith scored the winning goal as Arsenal delayed Manchester City's pursuit to the Women's Super League title by beating the leaders 1-0 at the Emirates Stadium.

items.title: ‘We lived a miraculous thing’: Castel di Sangro, 30 years on from their epic rise
items.summary: Small town club’s Serie B adventure captivated football and inspired a famous book. That spirit remains and is being passed to their successors The WhatsApp group flickers into life at about 6am every day. It is the manager who goes first because, when you are 79, old habits die hard. “Good morning,” Osvaldo Jaconi hails his former players and staff before, little by little, the salutations roll in from across Italy. Maybe it is someone’s birthday or another special occasion; the conversation may be accelerated by an in-joke that recalls why, three decades ago, they were brought together in the first place. Just in case anyone could forget, the group’s title says: “Serie B.” This is how miracles stay alive. Perhaps it is the point of what Castel di Sangro achieved in 1995-96. A rag-tag bunch from this backwater in mountainous Abruzzo had risen from local amateur leagues and then, in a crowning triumph with little precedent, made it to the second tier. “It’s like 30 years haven’t passed,” says Angelo Petrarca, who was nominally the masseur but often resembled a one-man backroom. “It shows how much love everybody has for each other, and did back then. As if everybody is still right here.” Continue reading...

items.title: How Ekitike has already become a Liverpool cult hero
items.summary: Inside the rise of Hugo Ekitike, adapting to life in England, connecting with Florian Wirtz and why he's already a fan favourite.
